skill.name: web-search
skill.description: Web search and content extraction with Tavily and Exa via inference.sh CLI
skill.summary: Web search and content extraction with Tavily and Exa APIs
skill.author: infsh-skills

security_audit.summary: All 38 static findings are false positives. External commands flagged are bash code blocks in markdown documentation showing CLI usage examples. Network URLs are expected documentation links for a web search tool. Weak crypto alerts misidentified YAML frontmatter and table content. No security risks detected - this is a documentation wrapper for the belt CLI tool.

medium_findings.title: Bash Commands in Documentation
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 24 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' in SKILL.md. Upon review, these are bash CLI command examples in markdown code blocks (e.g., 'belt app run tavily/search-assistant'). This is expected documentation for a CLI tool skill.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Bash commands are in markdown code blocks showing CLI usage examples, not actual executable code. No command injection vectors or dynamic execution exists.

low_findings.title: External URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 10 instances of 'Hardcoded URL' in SKILL.md. Upon review, these are legitimate documentation links: inference.sh platform URLs, example.com in sample queries, and GitHub raw content links for install instructions. Expected for a web search tool skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are expected documentation for a web search tool. All links point to legitimate inference.sh platform resources or example.com sample URLs.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Crypto False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 4 instances of 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at lines 3, 29, 36, 148. Upon review: line 3 is YAML frontmatter ('web-search' name), lines 29/36 are table headers, line 148 is a documentation URL. No cryptographic algorithms exist.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Static analyzer incorrectly identified YAML content, table formatting, and URLs as crypto algorithms. Semantic review confirms no cryptographic code exists.

content.user_title: Search the web with AI-powered Tavily and Exa
content.value_statement: Need current information from the web? This skill connects Claude to AI-powered search engines for real-time research and content extraction.
actual_capabilities: Search the web using Tavily AI-powered search engine
Search the web using Exa AI search
Get direct factual answers to questions
Extract and analyze web page content
Combine search with LLM analysis for research workflows
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI and API keys for Tavily/Exa
Results depend on search engine coverage and freshness
Content extraction may be blocked by some websites

use_cases.title: Research and Fact-Checking
use_cases.description: Search for current information and verify claims with real-time web search.

use_cases.title: RAG Pipelines
use_cases.description: Build retrieval-augmented generation workflows by searching and extracting web content for AI processing.

use_cases.title: Content Aggregation
use_cases.description: Collect information from multiple sources for analysis and summarization.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Web Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the web for [topic] using Tavily

prompt_templates.title: Extract Content
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the main content from these URLs: [url1], [url2]

prompt_templates.title: Research Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for recent information about [topic], then summarize the key findings

prompt_templates.title: Fact Verification
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for current information about [claim] and verify if it is accurate

output_examples.output: Returns AI-generated answers with sources and links to relevant articles
Returns clean text and structured data from the web page

output_examples.input: What is the population of Tokyo?
output_examples.output: Returns direct factual answer: approximately 37 million people

best_practices: Use specific search queries for more relevant results
Combine search with LLM analysis for research workflows
Store search results for reference before they change
anti_patterns: Do not use for real-time breaking news (search APIs have latency)
Do not assume all websites allow content extraction
Do not use for private or authenticated content access

faq.question: What API keys do I need?
faq.answer: You need Tavily and Exa API keys from inference.sh

faq.question: Is this free to use?
faq.answer: API calls to Tavily and Exa have usage limits and pricing

faq.question: Can I search any website?
faq.answer: Most public websites can be searched but some may block extraction

faq.question: How fresh are the search results?
faq.answer: Results depend on search engine index freshness, typically hours to days

faq.question: Can I extract content from paywalled sites?
faq.answer: Content extraction only works on publicly accessible pages

faq.question: How does this compare to direct API calls?
faq.answer: This skill provides a convenient CLI wrapper around the search APIs
